description_long: This kiosk consists of a series of curved and angled display panels arranged on a flat, polygonal base. The panels are primarily white with red borders and feature a variety of printed graphics, including botanical illustrations and diagrams, with a prominent central panel displaying large white flowers on a red background. The structure appears open and accessible from multiple sides, with no enclosed walls or roof, and includes several freestanding triangular and rectangular signboards. The materials suggest lightweight construction, likely using printed boards or laminated panels supported by a simple frame. The overall design is modular and visually engaging, suitable for temporary exhibitions or informational displays in public spaces. The kiosk is designed to attract attention and facilitate interaction with the displayed content.
description: A modular, open kiosk with curved and angled display panels featuring botanical graphics and diagrams, set on a flat base. The structure is lightweight, visually engaging, and accessible from multiple sides.
description_view_0: From this view, the central red panel with large white flowers is prominent, flanked by curved and angled display boards with printed graphics.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the long, curved display panel with red borders and white background, as well as a freestanding rectangular signboard in the background.
description_view_2: The central floral panel is seen head-on, with the curved display wrapping around and additional triangular signboards visible.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the rear of the kiosk, showing the back of the curved display and the arrangement of freestanding signboards.
attribute_signage_type: Printed signs and banners integrated into the display panels, featuring botanical illustrations and diagrams; no digital or illuminated signage visible.
attribute_roof_design: none; open-air with no visible roof structure.
attribute_architectural_style: Modern exhibition style with minimalist, modular influences and a focus on visual communication.
